body_txt: In the real world, Elvis Presley died on August 16, 1977 at the age of 42. In common fiction, and in the minds of some in reality , Elvis is not dead. Whether through conspiracy, alien abduction (and later return), or retirement , he's still on Earth (or a variation thereof ). See Also: Elvis Has Left the Planet , where he is outside Earth. Slowly became a Discredited Trope as the years passed into what would have been Elvis' old age (he was born on Jan. 8 1935, so as of 2026, if Elvis were still alive he would be 91) though it would persist by depicting him as an immortal or in the custody of aliens with life-extending technology. Even if he were in the bloom of life, though, the music scene has moved on without him, and he wouldn't be the medium-defining talent he once was. The concept has also been applied to more recent celebrities whose rabid fans could not accept their abrupt deaths, including Tupac Shakur (compounded by the huge amount of recorded material he left, enough to fill quite a few posthumous albums), The Notorious B.I.G. , hide , Michael Jackson and Whitney Houston . On the other hand, check out the story below of the folk rocker Rodriguez , with whom this actually happened! And for an inversion, contrast the "Paul is dead" conspiracy theories regarding Paul McCartney . Hilariously enough, this trope has given rise to a common misconception about Elvis’ death: many non-Americans and people who were too young to remember Elvis, seeing jokes about him being in hiding, assume that the King disappeared without a trace like Amelia Earhart , instead of being found dead of a heart attack in the bathroom.
